steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$\frac{ \left(x+9\right) \left(x-9\right)}{x+9}=7$$Graph or solve it directly and read the x-value that works. The requested value is $16$.

steps.title: Cancel the common factor
steps.content: The numerator and denominator both contain $x+9$. Since none of the choices is $-9$, the expression simplifies safely for the answer choices: $$\frac{(x+9)(x-9)}{x+9}=x-9$$

steps.title: Solve the simpler equation
steps.content: So solve $$x-9=7$$ Add $9$ to both sides: $$x=16$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C (‘$16$’) is $16$. Substitution gives $\frac{(25)(7)}{25}=7$, so it works. The other choices give values that are not $7$.
